name: Anonymous
comment:
>>530189426
That's not necessarily a great indicator. Lots of times you can see that something is dying when it just peaked (i.e. plenty of people knew cable tv was dead when streaming was just starting to catch on)

Individual genres always have their own issues, but the broad problem with videogames isn't so much a lack of depth, but a lack of meaningful novelty. When we actually see well executed novel ideas, games can be big hits (i.e. rocket league), but by definition there's not many of these ideas possible, and what happens is the market reacts and saturates the genre, or publishers realize there's no point in releasing a new "me too" against an established leader. The other thing that makes games worth playing is mechanics that stand the test of time, but this is a very small percentage, probably far below what most people expect (most consoles have 0-5 games designed to this level).
